reviews.text: Doug, Liam and Melissa I'd definitely recommend to someone if they ever needed a injury Lawyer! After getting into a car Accident in March, I thought I was able to handle things my way not thinking I'd have to lawyer up. The insurance at fault wasn't doing anything to help after having my only mode of transportation taken away from me I couldn't even get a rental. After asking 3 times and being rejected I was offered a few measly dollars to settle my case. After hearing the amount I felt offended be cause at this point the insurance didn't care for my well being so I went lawyer shopping. Called a few a of the big names advertised around town to get some information and found out how slick of of them are by saying 'We are gonna email you some documents for you to read and sign and we'll get started on your case.' Not explaining what the process will be. After speaking with Liam he was able to give me a break down on the details and how this could be a lengthy process so it will take a lot of patience on my end. Liam was able to set me up with Doug that was going to take on my case and that's if I chose to have Gerber represent me. I'm glad I made the choice! From Melissa to Liam to Doug I was kept in the loop at all times. We got to point to where my case hit a snag. I wasn't getting better my finances was pretty much exhausted. The Team was able to assist me in getting help and treatment that I didn't know I needed. For 6 to 7 months I didn't know I was walking around with a fracture in my leg that required surgery. Doug and his team was able to find me a Doctor willing to take on my case working on a Lein so that I wouldn't have to come out of pocket. Again I was kept in the loop at all times and I got the surgery and treatment I needed. I was very happy they way I was represented and taken care of by Gerber. Just make sure to be safe and buckle up when driving. Also keep in mind accidents happen.

Ryan was always extremely available for our numerous questions which removed the stress of the situation. Ryan was able to close our transaction timely and I recommend his services to all.

After 5 calls and emails without a response I got a referral from the realtor for the estate who put me in touch with Ryan.  We had the initial consult, decided on a less complicated way to deal with the issue and 3 days later the documents were prepared.  I could not happier with the service I received.  Thanks again Ryan.

My mother passed away without a will so Jackson and White was hired to deal with her affairs to pass the assets through probate. This is a very long and frustrating process, and having an attorney to facilitate the process is critical. The attorney filed all the court paperwork and facilitated communication with the other heir in order to get everything signed and submitted for court approval on time. In addition, all my questions and follow ups were answered promptly, of which there were many. I feel that the cost incurred to use an attorney versus navigating the probate process oneself is well worth the money. Personal thanks to Ryan Hodges for his diligence, patience and professional demeanor in what took dramatically longer (through no fault of the firm) than it should have. Ryan has my endorsement as an asset to the firm and an enormous help in this process. Thanks for everything.

Rohlwing wanted $5000 up front. I wanted a $2500/$2500 split if defense was successful.
It was difficult to get him to do anything proactive.
Continuance after continuance seemed to be his modus operendi.
It was an assault charge involving a female victim case assigned to a female judge.
I had to do 6 most at tent city during the Summer.
The one thing Rohlwing did was securing work release.
After speaking with another counselor at the time he told me he could have gotten me jail time for $1000.
I laughed but was a bit perturbed at his sincerity.
This all happened back in 1995 or 96.
It is good to see that Rohlwing seems to be doing well but know this:
I am an SMU graduate and had never been charged with any crime.
I am a bitter, however all is good and forgiveness is important.
Sincerely, David C. Mills
Best lawyer He is wonderful and surprisingly not as expensive as lawyers as good as him he's awesome he's been great to me
Have used him twice and must say he is a fantastic lawyer. Definitely would refer him to a friend...

I hired Gary to defend me and he took my money then bailed he barely communicated with me  for over 6 months we had maybe 3 conversations on the phone which I had to consecutively bug him to get that and then he showed up to my trial unprepared and without words for the trail. The countless times he said ummm and didn't even question the other party and the questions he did give to ill witnesses who should have been non existent because of underlying reason he did nothing! He was basically asking for a pay day with minimal effort i would have been better off with a public defender and not wasting my savings I guarantee you will feel the same I  would not recommend! Also fins it funny how all the low ratings keep disappearing from his reviews on Google all say the same he takes ur money n runs! Just look out!!!
Mr. Rohlwing is a great lawyer...  He is determined to get the best for you and does not rush things just to close a case.
Gary went above and beyond helping me with my set aside during the COVID pandemic. He did great work!

When other attorneys would not give us the time of day, Mr Rohlwing was honest about how legally weak our circumstances yielded, and he came through for us anyway, in a timely manner.
The guy is a genius!

I’ve used Gary on numerous occasions and his advice and representation is always spot on.
Gary always puts his clients best interest first you never feel like the clock is running and you’re going to be charged for every second you speak with him.
His vast knowledge of law always impresses me no matter what the circumstances have been.
I would highly recommend using Gary as your attorney you won’t be disappointed.

Gary came through for me in the time that i thought it couldn't be done. He helped me with a case outside of the county he was very professional and helpful.I would recommend him to anyone looking for legal representation. Keep up the great work Gary!

Almost had my home seized by park management and Gary took care of that also, even though it was out of his scope of practice. No charge for the extra help. Would recommend him to anyone looking for a Attorney for criminal charges and to get Justice.  Good communication and friendly and no BS. Appreciate the honesty.

BEGINNING: This is only the start.  Pastor told me get membership with Legal Shied.  Waste of my money. Called the following day. Referred me to a attorney in Tempe. Said I would have to fax police report to them before they even send the referral.  Waste of time and money. Will cancel that membership.  Called Gary and left message and he called me back in 10 minutes.  Have a appointment tomorrow free consultation and willing to work with me on a payment plan.  So first impression is great.  Will keep adding reviews after meeting and see where this goes. Facing Domestic violence charges and disorderly conduct from a woman I said could stay a short time until she got paid on Friday. Found put she had no job and I asked her to leave with her two children.  Called the police and said I pushed her and she was my roommate.  Taken to jail and also kicked out of my home that I own in a manufactured park. Don't own the land. Lived there 11 years. Home is paid off. Took me to court on the eviction I had no attorney and between the prosecutor,  The police, The park management they ate me alive. Had 24 hours to vacate. Police lied in court. Never questioned my witness that was at my home when all this transpired.  Objected to every word that came out of my mouth. Now need to sell home ASAP or they will put a lean on it and take ownership. So I am homeless.
GRAY IS THE BEST!!!!!

Gary was not only knowledgeable and helpful but was quick to let me know when and where to be to walk me through the entire process.  Thank you Gary for relieving a lot of stress during a stressful time.

If you want the best, don’t call the rest! Call Paul Dombeck.
My wife was recently denied twice for SSDI (at the Initial and Reconsideration Level). She then requested a Hearing before an Administrative Law Judge and wanted attorney representation for that Level. We both then took on the task of finding an attorney to represent her. Between us, we called 15+ attorneys (local and national) to see if they would take my wife’s case. ALL 15+ attorneys declined because of the unique circumstances surrounding the case and my wife’s rare medical condition. We almost gave up and were very concerned my wife would have to go before a judge without legal representation. It was then we found Paul Dombeck by chance. He was highly knowledgeable of the SSDI claims process and compassionate about my wife’s medical condition. We dealt with both Paul and Tatiana leading up to the Hearing, and they were both quick to answer any questions we had. Paul did an excellent job explaining the Hearing process to put my wife’s mind at ease. Long story short, my wife received a Fully Favorable Decision, and that was all down to Paul and Tatiana. We both can’t thank them enough. If my wife and I ever have the need for an attorney in the future, Paul will be our go-to-guy!
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Mark. We're delighted to hear about your wife's successful SSDI case! Your determination led you here, and we're thrilled that Paul and Tatiana's efforts and compassion made all the difference. Your trust in us is greatly appreciated, and we're here for you whenever you need legal support in the future!

Ongoing Ethical Violations, Procedural Abuse, and Active Harm to a Disabled Man

I am writing this review based on my direct involvement in an active probate and eviction case in which Joseph Charles represents the estate of my late grandfather. What is happening in this case is not theoretical, historical, or resolved — it is actively causing severe emotional distress, financial devastation, and catastrophic medical risk to my family.

In my experience, Mr. Charles’ handling of this matter demonstrates deep ethical concerns, procedural abuse, and alarming professional incompetence.

This case involves a contested probate estate with multiple pending disputes, including a verified petition to remove the Personal Representative for misconduct, as well as unresolved issues involving estate mismanagement, medical neglect, and fiduciary violations. Despite these serious legal issues, Mr. Charles is aggressively pursuing an eviction action in Justice Court against an heir who is not a tenant, has no lease, pays no rent, and occupies the property solely to protect and preserve estate assets.

There is no landlord–tenant relationship whatsoever, yet eviction court is being weaponized to force removal of heirs while probate disputes remain unresolved.

A formal Motion to Dismiss for lack of jurisdiction and procedural defects was filed, raising critical legal failures, including:

• Improper and defective service of the statutory 10-day demand to vacate
• Misidentification of parties and improper naming of “all occupants”
• Jurisdictional defects due to the complete absence of any tenancy relationship
• Statutory violations limiting the Personal Representative’s authority while a removal action is pending

Despite these fundamental defects, the motion was denied, and a writ of restitution was issued on February 11, 2026, placing my father — a severely disabled man with advanced spinal degeneration, fractures, nerve damage, and failing hip hardware — at immediate risk of homelessness.

My father cannot walk, is wheelchair dependent, and suffers constant debilitating pain. Forcing him from his home under these conditions is medically dangerous and potentially irreversible. This eviction is actively causing extreme emotional distress, financial devastation, and irreparable medical harm.

To make matters worse, Mr. Charles has repeatedly mischaracterized me as a lawyer or paralegal simply because I am capable of researching statutes and preparing legal documents. I have no legal education or legal training whatsoever. His inability to correctly understand or verify even basic professional distinctions is deeply troubling and reflects a disturbing lack of competence and diligence — especially for an attorney entrusted with complex probate and eviction litigation.

This repeated misrepresentation further highlights how little attention he pays to facts, accuracy, and procedural integrity — deficiencies that directly impact vulnerable people in high-stakes legal situations.

In my opinion, this case demonstrates a pattern of aggressive litigation tactics, misuse of eviction court, disregard for probate protections, and indifference to human consequences. Eviction court is being used to bypass probate jurisdiction, silence heirs, and force displacement of a medically fragile man, rather than resolve disputes properly through the probate court system.

This case is actively ongoing, and the harm is happening right now.

I strongly caution anyone considering hiring Joseph Charles to carefully research his litigation practices and history, especially in probate, estate, and eviction matters involving families, medical hardship, and vulnerable individuals.

Alex made the time to fit me in right away for an actual sit-down, then made me feel like he had all the time in the world to discuss my situation. I cannot say enough about his sincere concern for my needs and compassionate understanding of my ordeal. I only wish I had found him sooner! It would have made my life so much easier! No question he will be my first call from now on. I Highly Recommend Alex, and his team, for anyone in need of a skilled, professional attorney who simply is - the Real Deal!

reviews.text: Urgent Law and Alex Moorhead provided stellar service addressing our legal needs quickly and efficiently and, we have returned for additional services since and had the same great experience.  We never felt hurried along and Alex patiently guided us through all the legal "mumbo jumbo" to ensure we understood completely what we were doing and signing.  I'm a stickler for customer service and follow-thru and with Alex and Urgent Law we got both in spades.  We strongly recommend Urgent Law and Alex Moorhead.
